title: The push flow encrypts operation batches, uploads them to Bee /bytes, and updates per-document feeds plus drive and user manifests.
content:
## Push sequence (Create to Sync to Swarm)

Triggered by `SyncManager.updateOutbox()` when new ordinals appear in `operation_index_operations`.

`SwarmChannel.handleOutboxAdded()` then, per SyncOperation:

1. Serialize `OperationWithContext[]` to JSON.
2. Encrypt with AES-256-GCM using a wallet-derived key.
3. Upload ciphertext to `/bytes` — receive content reference (hash).
4. Read existing document manifest from feed (or create new).
5. Append the batch reference (with scope and index range) to the manifest.
6. Write updated manifest as a SOC (single-owner chunk) to the doc feed.
7. Call `syncOp.executed()` so the sync cursor advances.

### Special case: drive documents

When `documentType === "powerhouse/document-drive"`:

- `ManifestManager.updateDriveManifest()` reads drive state from the reactor and writes the docs plus folders index to the drive manifest feed.
- `ManifestManager.ensureDriveInUserManifest()` updates the user manifest with a drive entry, building the discovery index used for recovery.

### Example operation set

Creating `PushPullDrive` with one doc, one folder, one nested doc generates: `CREATE_DOCUMENT`, `UPGRADE_DOCUMENT`, `ADD_FILE`, `ADD_FOLDER`, `ADD_FILE (parentFolder=folder)`, `MOVE_NODE`.
description: SwarmChannel serializes ops, AES-256-GCM encrypts with wallet-derived key, uploads to /bytes, and appends batch references into document, drive, and user manifest feeds.
